_meta.description: REAL capture, converted to DetectionEvent shapes. Do not hand-edit the events without updating this file's source pointers below.
_meta.session: logged-in (adult account) — the only session this video was captured in
_meta.note: IMPORTANT — encodes reality, does not fabricate: the real capture's playabilityStatusValue is 'OK' in every record (the observer was logged in as an adult, per RESULTS.md §2's 'session: logged-in (adult)'; RESULTS.md §3 finding 6 notes 'Age-restricted (logged-in adult): zero placements' as the observed behavior). There is NO logged-out capture of this video in any spike export, so no record ever carried playabilityStatus LOGIN_REQUIRED for it. Per the task's own conditional instruction, since the real data does not contain that variant, this fixture does NOT synthesize a LOGIN_REQUIRED/UNAVAILABLE case for this video — fixtures.test.ts asserts only the real NO_ADS-shaped absence. Generic (non-fixture-backed) playabilityStatus!=='OK' -> UNAVAILABLE coverage already exists in classifier.test.ts.
omittedFromExport: No dom_ad_event records exist for this video in the raw export (verified: zero DOM records with watchUrlVideoId JM1G0BXHQyU) — 'none' DOM ads played, matching RESULTS.md's 'absent' / 'none' row. Nothing trimmed there; there is no DOM evidence to include.

Fetch-path noise immediately following the 'initial' read (pageVideoId=JM1G0BXHQyU but a different/absent videoId): three records at 2026-07-02T21:00:01.804Z-21:00:01.811Z with videoId=null and note='no videoDetails' (malformed/prefetch reads with no videoDetails at all), one with videoId='ux' (2-character garbage from a non-canonical sibling endpoint — the /youtubei/v1/player/ad_break-class issue classifier.ts's summarizePlacementCounts doc comment describes), and one with an 11-character videoId (4qnNRUoV5Ag at 21:00:01.820Z) that is an ad creative's OWN player response through the identical /youtubei/v1/player endpoint. All real but exactly what the real pipeline drops: classify() and background.ts's handleIncomingEvent both filter on videoId !== pageVideoId (or a missing videoId) before an event would ever reach a session — omitted here for the identical reason, not fabricated absence.
conversionNotes: No BEACON events captured by the Phase 0 spike tool (see green-mrbeast-iYlODtkyw_I.json).
_meta.expectedClassification: playabilityStatus is 'OK' and all evidence is absent (no placements, no DOM, no beacon). With a valid, non-rewatch (fresh) observer -> NO_ADS, matching RESULTS.md's observed 'zero placements' for this special case.
